title: Python Execution, Tools, and Evidence · Contract Workshop
summary: Build and diagnose a small command-line learning log across REPL, script, editor, and terminal workflows. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a reproducible command-line learning log while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain how source, the interpreter, objects, and standard streams participate when a Python program runs.
Choose REPL, script, module, or notebook execution based on the task and reproducibility needs.
Read syntax errors and tracebacks from the final exception back to the relevant source cause.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
